method: L2 token-CLASS positional structure. Classified corpus 'other' tokens as LOGOGRAM (raw starts with '*') or FRACTION (raw contains fraction slash U+2044 or a vulgar-fraction char U+2150..U+215F). Two positional metrics: H1 LOGO->NUM = fraction of logogram tokens immediately followed by a 'num' token; H2 FRAC-TERMINAL = fraction of fraction tokens immediately followed by 'nl' or END. Unified null: within each line (segment of non-NL tokens between NL tokens), randomly permute the order of non-NL tokens preserving the line's token multiset and NL/line structure; recompute H1 and H2; 1000 permutations; perm p = frac(null >= observed) one-sided enrichment. Positive control (synthetic) gates the verdict: DETECT (planted logo-before-num + frac-line-final) must flag both; FALSE-POSITIVE (uniform placement within variable-length lines) must not fire above 0.10 across 30 draws. Cross-site: per site with >=10 logograms (H1) / >=10 fractions (H2).
key_findings: H1 LOGO->NUM globally enriched: 58.1% of logogram-class tokens are immediately followed by a numeral, vs 25.9% under within-line position-shuffle null (perm p=0.0, 1000 perms). Commodity-sign-then-quantity is a real positional regularity, not a within-line ordering artifact.

H2 FRAC-TERMINAL globally enriched: 99.0% of fraction-class tokens are entry-terminal (immediately followed by nl or END), vs 46.1% under null (perm p=0.0). The fractional remainder closes the ledger entry essentially without exception.

Cross-site consistency: H1 enriched in 3/3 testable sites (Haghia Triada p=0.0, Khania p=0.0, Phaistos p=0.018); H2 enriched in 3/3 testable sites (Haghia Triada p=0.0, Khania p=0.0, Zakros p=0.0). The template replicates across geographically distinct findspots.

Positive control PASSED: synthetic detect corpus (logo always before num, frac always line-final) flagged both at p=0.0; uniform-placement false-positive rate = 0.10 (at bound, <=0.10). Machinery is informative.

The combined positional template is: [commodity-logogram] -> [numeral quantity] ... [fraction remainder | line-end]. Token CLASS + POSITION only; no logogram identity, no fraction value, no readings used.
successor_hypotheses: E061: Does the logogram->numeral adjacency hold at a FIXED distance (logo immediately before num) vs allowing one intervening token (e.g. a divider)? Test distance-1 vs distance-2 logo->num enrichment.
E062: Is the fraction-terminal position tied to a preceding numeral (num->frac->nl triple) more than chance? Test whether num->frac adjacency is itself enriched beyond the within-line null.
E063: Do entries with a logogram also tend to contain a fraction (logo-entry vs frac-entry co-occurrence at the line/entry level), suggesting a single commodity+quantity+remainder entry type?

E064: Are there entry-level positional SLOTS (a fixed small set of class-positions per line, e.g. [logo][num][frac]) recoverable by an L2 slot-grammar / HMM over token classes, beyond pairwise adjacencies?

E065: Does the fraction-terminal regularity extend to the Linear A native fraction signs (A701-A732, U+10740-U+10755) currently classified OTHERSIGN, i.e. are those also entry-terminal? (Would unify the two fraction notations positionally.)

E066: Cross-site variation in the logo->num rate (HT 0.62, Khania 0.43, Phaistos 0.50): is the deficit at Khania/Phaistos explained by logograms followed by DIV (divider) then num, i.e. a site-specific slot variant?

E067: Test the entry template on the Linear B (LB) corpus as a NEGATIVE/sanity control: LB PC synthetic only (LB stream lacks this exact class tagging); confirm the machinery does not fabricate the template where it should not exist.
non_circular: Token CLASSES derived solely from corpus tagging (t=='other' + raw pattern: '*' prefix => LOGOGRAM; U+2044 slash or U+2150..U+215F vulgar-fraction char => FRACTION) and POSITION only. No logogram identity (*304 = which commodity), no fraction value (1/2 vs 1/4), no readings, no phonetics, no semantics, no metrological arithmetic used in either metric or null. The null permutes within-line token order preserving the multiset, so any commodity/quantity association from sign identity is destroyed; only positional class structure is tested. L2 ONLY.
deviations: False-positive control rate landed exactly at the 0.10 bound (3/30 draws flagged under uniform placement); this is at-bound not below, driven by the coarseness of permutation p-values for short lines. The detect arm is unambiguous (p=0.0). Verdict proceeds since fp_ok is defined as <=0.10.
LB positive control is synthetic only (LB stream lacks the 'other'+raw class tagging needed for logogram/fraction classification); stated per protocol.
